title: 第四組_祖母與白壁虎
body:
Grandma: This secret project took them well over a decade.
Grandma: ...
Grandma: It's been way too long.
Grandma: ...
Grandma: The progress was slow, not because of shortage, of either talent or funding.
Grandma: It's just that...
Grandma: The lack of a vision.
Grandma: They need to be driven forward. I left Nin and you alone to act as the driving force that they need.
//白壁虎現行，被靠著欄杆，雙手掛在上面(跩跩的感覺)
Camleon Bai: You need someone to give Vision its daily dose of insecurity, huh.
Camleon Bai: Me and Situ, we happened to fit the bill.
Camleon Bai: Ha. Well played, granny.
Grandma: ...
Camleon Bai: You knew Situ planned to blow this place up, didn't you? Don't tell me you were not worried?
Grandma: I trust you. In that case, you would salvage research data and sell it to one of Vision's competitors.
Grandma: Meanwhile, Vision would scramble to rebuild and catch up.
Camleon Bai: What if it can't catch up?
Grandma: That means someone else must have found the holy grail.
Grandma: I have no problem either way.
Camleon Bai: ...
Camleon Bai: ...What do you want from this?
Grandma: ...
Grandma: Since the first human was born, we were assigned a purpose.
Grandma: What is it? Generations of philosophers have made countless speculations. No one could say for sure.
Grandma: I'm just trying to answer that question, looking at the big picture from miles above.
Camleon Bai: Heh. I don't understand a single word of yours.
//這是第一次祖母看向白壁虎，祖母轉頭(要WAIT停頓一下)，然後白壁虎也轉過去看向祖母
Grandma: Oh, you understand it well enough.
Grandma: Don't bother playing dumb.
Camleon Bai: ...
Camleon Bai: One last question. How did you know I'm here?;//白壁虎把頭轉回去
Grandma: I know you well enough to predict your next move.;//祖母轉頭回去
Camleon Bai: Ha! One of your agents said exactly that.
Camleon Bai: Good night, granny.
Grandma: Good night.
<<#End>>

title: 第一組_加入鴞隊
body: //(不管有沒有司徒) Frances Cheng: In the end you still have to join OWL. Jason Dai: Sorry, I shouldn't have— Frances Cheng: There's nothing to be sorry for, haha! Frances Cheng: I just meant to say the world didn't give us many choices. It's not your fault. Jason Dai: I, um... Jason Dai: I really hate to make you a caged bird. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Wake up, Mr. Dai! Frances Cheng: Stop blaming yourself for one second! Jason Dai: But— Frances Cheng: I don't see myself as a caged bird. Frances Cheng: This is kind of...exciting. Jason Dai: ...? Frances Cheng: Huh? Why are you looking at me like that? Frances Cheng: I've been thinking about what Nin said. Frances Cheng: It might look like we're wearing shackles. Frances Cheng: But these shackles have given us a lot of power. Jason Dai: Us? Frances Cheng: Right! Technically you are the OWL agent, but we're a team! Frances Cheng: Remember what that mercenary guy told you? Jason Dai: ...Chameleon? Jason Dai: Yeah. Frances Cheng: Back then he said... Frances Cheng: You're a player now. Make your own moves. Jason Dai: He was just messing with my mind. Frances Cheng: I think he's making perfect sense. Frances Cheng: We walked into a new world, with a high cost... Frances Cheng: From now on, let's make our own moves, together. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Yeah, together. Frances Cheng: Together! <<#Wait(1)>> Jason Dai: So...;<> Frances Cheng: Hmm?;<> Jason Dai: Um... Frances Cheng: Something's wrong? Jason Dai: N-no! It's just—;<> Jason Dai: It's just that I ca— //程芳拿出這個 Frances Cheng: You can't find this?;<> Jason Dai: What?? Fran?? How did you—;<> <> Frances Cheng: Tsk. Jason Dai. Watch and learn.;//程芳打開盒子 Frances Cheng: I love you. I love every second we've spent together, of sadness and joy, through thick and thin. Frances Cheng: So, will you marry me? Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: YES!;<>;<> Jason Dai: Even if someone's pointing a gun at my head I'd still say yes! //程芳打一下主角的頭 Frances Cheng: Hey! Why would anyone do that?;<> Jason Dai: Haha, just saying. Jason Dai: For real, when did you steal that ring from me?;<> Frances Cheng: Didn't I tell you Nin taught me some spy tricks? Frances Cheng: So I found your gift and stole it, I'm the winner of this anniversary! Jason Dai: Let's try again next year! Jason Dai: The first anniversary of wedding.;<> Frances Cheng: Sure thing! Hee hee. <<#End>>

title: 第一組_逃亡
body: //程芳一個人坐在沙發上講電話 Frances Cheng: Yes, I see... Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: OK, good. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I'm alright. Frances Cheng: I mean, really! You don't need to come by! Frances Cheng: You must be busy. Don't let me interrupt your work! Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I know, and I believe you. Frances Cheng: But... Frances Cheng: If we do manage to locate Jason, you will have a problem, no? Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Don't worry. I understand. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Thank you, Nin. Frances Cheng: I know you have tried. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: It's not your fault at all. Jason made his own decision. Frances Cheng: I will... Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: No, never mind... Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Thank you. Good night. //程芳掛了電話 Frances Cheng: ... //等一下以後，程芳站起來 Frances Cheng: ...I will make my own decision. Frances Cheng: Do you mind, Jason? Frances Cheng: It's my turn now... Frances Cheng: I will find you, no matter where you are. Frances Cheng: I promise. <<#End>>

title: 第一組_炸工廠
body:
//乘方站在陽台或是拍在客廳桌子上睡覺
//一開始焦距沒有拍到他，最後在TWEEN過去
//窗外都是警報聲、救護車聲、人聲吵雜
TV: It's been a week since the Great Blackout, but the chaos is not yet abating.
TV: Here we are, at the site of the 11th ship collision accident.
TV: As you can see, cargo ship AI-4G4, owned by Growell, rammed into the elevation framework beneath Rui'an District.
TV: The entire district is tilting toward one side. Local residents are being evacuated as we speak.
TV: Meanwhile, many hospitals are paralyzed due to dysfunctional automated systems. Those injured in the accident are not receiving the medical care they desperately need.
TV: As a result, mortality rate is quickly rising.
TV: The cause of this incident, and the chaos that ensued, however, remains a mystery.
TV: According to a recent statement from Vision Corporation...
TV: The Great Blackout was caused by a series of terrorist attacks targeting Vision flop farms.
TV: That is to be verified, as the police is working vigorously on the case.
<<#End>>

title: 第二組_雙贏
body:
Quentin Qiu: Don't you guys think it weird to see Wayne in that chair?
Tyler Cao: The chair and the man, they don't belong to the same room.
Wayne Zheng: Right...
Cathy Hsieh: Don't say that about your division lead!
Jeff Wu: Uh-uh, not division lead anymore. He's the manager now.
Tyler Cao: Shall we call him Mr. Zheng?
Cathy Hsieh: That feels weird alright...
Quentin Qiu: See?
Jeff Wu: Stop it, you guys. You're hurting Wayne's feelings!
Wayne Zheng: My feelings are doing well enough, haha...
George Liao: You're the manager now, Mr. Manager. Act like a manager.
Quentin Qiu: Don't cram so many managers into one sentence! You're butchering the language! Haha.
George Liao: Well, he is the manager, isn't he?
//俊恩進來
<<Set($DLState,後日談_俊恩進來)>>
Wayne Zheng: ...
Justin Wu: ...
Quentin Qiu: Wow, buzzkill.
Cathy Hsieh: <s>Shut up for a second, QQ!</s>
Justin Wu: ...
Justin Wu: Congrats, new manager.
Wayne Zheng: Thank you, Justin!
Justin Wu: Heh. It's weird to see you in that chair.
Wayne Zheng: Hahaha!
Quentin Qiu: Ha! What did I say!?
//賴潔胖胖陳家騏也跑進來
<<Set($DLState,後日談_賴潔胖胖陳家騏進來)>>
Jesse Lai: So, this is the manager's office?
Kaylee Chen: I've never been here before.
Paxton Luo: Disappointingly small.
Wayne Zheng: Hey folks! Hold on!
//孫茹跟何歆翊進來
<<Set($DLState,後日談_孫茹跟何歆翊進來)>>
Cynthia Ho: Hey, hey! Congrats from me and Rose!
Rose Sun: How does it feel, being the big boss?
Wayne Zheng: This room is running out of room!
Rose Sun: Does that mean we're not welcome?
Wayne Zheng: Um, I don't mean—
Quentin Qiu: You occupy too much room, muscle mountain.
Rose Sun: Says the lard ball.
Cathy Hsieh: Hey, guys!
Cathy Hsieh: Where's Jason?
Quentin Qiu: Right! I haven't heard from Jayster in a while. What's he up to?
Tyler Cao: Wayne?
Wayne Zheng: ...
Wayne Zheng: Jason resigned.
Quentin Qiu: Huh!?
Justin Wu: Are you kidding?
Wayne Zheng: For real. He didn't give me a reason.

Tyler Cao: ...
Cathy Hsieh: No way...
Quentin Qiu: Jayster...
//if有幫采欣球球成功
<<#If($BallTsaiShinEventStatus=4)>>
    Quentin Qiu: He helped me, like, a lot.
    Cathy Hsieh: ...
<<#EndIf>>
Jeff Wu: ...
//if廖哥小峰成功
<<#If($FonLiauEventStatus=2)>>
    George Liao: ...I owe him a dinner.
    Jeff Wu: Ha, maybe he just wanted to avoid the dinner.
<<#EndIf>>
Justin Wu: Have you tried to contact him?
Wayne Zheng: I have, but he's not responding.
Kaylee Chen: He must have his reasons.
Jesse Lai: Say, he's kind of fishy from the very beginning. A man with secrets.
//if陳家騏事件成功
<<#If($SQ_LaijieJiaChi=3)>>
    Kaylee Chen: But I'm sure he's a good guy.
    Jesse Lai: Right, a nice guy, at least.
<<#EndIf>>
Paxton Luo: I thought we could be friends...
Rose Sun: Come on! We're not holding a wake! He's not dead!
Cynthia Ho: Right! The world is not that big. Sooner or later we will run into him again!
Cathy Hsieh: Exactly!
Wayne Zheng: Thanks to him, this farm has a happy ending, better than we could ever ask for.
Justin Wu: True.
Jeff Wu: Hopefully I could run into him sooner.
Quentin Qiu: With an employment market like that? Pretty soon he'll come back begging for a job!
Cathy Hsieh: That's not a nice thing to say!
Rose Sun: Ha! But it makes sense!
Wayne Zheng: Let's work together and make Silencio a much, much better farm.
Wayne Zheng: If only to give Jason a surprise when he comes back!
Justin Wu: That's a great idea.
Wayne Zheng: Let's do that, friends!
<<#End>>

title: 第二組_篩選贏
body:
Quentin Qiu: Don't you guys think it weird to see Wayne in that chair?
Tyler Cao: The chair and the man, they don't belong to the same room.
Wayne Zheng: Right...
Cathy Hsieh: Don't say that about your division lead!
Jeff Wu: Uh-uh, not division lead anymore. He's the manager now.
Tyler Cao: Shall we call him Mr. Zheng?
Cathy Hsieh: That feels weird alright...
Quentin Qiu: See?
Jeff Wu: Stop it, you guys. You're hurting Wayne's feelings!
Wayne Zheng: My feelings are doing well enough, haha...
George Liao: You're the manager now, Mr. Manager. Act like a manager.
Quentin Qiu: Don't cram so many managers into one sentence! You're butchering the language! Haha.
George Liao: Well, he is the manager, isn't he?
//俊恩進來
<<Set($DLState,後日談_俊恩進來)>>
Wayne Zheng: ...
Justin Wu: ...
Justin Wu: I'm leaving, Wayne.
Wayne Zheng: Don't. Silencio needs you.
Justin Wu: No.
Justin Wu: Silencio needs to be shut down.
Wayne Zheng: ...Looks like I can't change your mind.
Justin Wu: And I can't change yours. I should leave. It's better for us both.
Wayne Zheng: ...
Justin Wu: Whatever you do next, wish you the best of luck.
Wayne Zheng: Thank you.
Justin Wu: Bye, Wane.
Wayne Zheng: Bye, Justin.
//俊恩出去
<<Set($DLState,後日談_俊恩出去)>>
Wayne Zheng: ...
Quentin Qiu: There goes the poop face...
Cathy Hsieh: QQ!!!
Quentin Qiu: Sorry. Bad choice of words...
Wayne Zheng: ...
Cathy Hsieh: Hey, guys!
Cathy Hsieh: Where's Jason?
Quentin Qiu: Right! I haven't heard from Jayster in a while. What's he up to?
Tyler Cao: Wayne?
Wayne Zheng: ...
Wayne Zheng: Jason resigned.
Quentin Qiu: Huh?
Tyler Cao: Are you kidding?
Wayne Zheng: For real. He didn't give me a reason.
George Liao: ...
Cathy Hsieh: No way...
Quentin Qiu: Jayster...
//if有幫采欣球球
<<#If($BallTsaiShinEventStatus=4)>>
    Quentin Qiu: He helped me, like, a lot.
    Cathy Hsieh: ...
<<#EndIf>>
Jeff Wu: ...
//if
<<#If($FonLiauEventStatus=2)>>
    George Liao: ...I owe him a dinner.
    Jeff Wu: Ha, maybe he just wanted to avoid the dinner.
<<#EndIf>>
Tyler Cao: Have you tried to contact him?
Wayne Zheng: I have, but he's not responding.
Jeff Wu: He must have his reasons.
Tyler Cao: I always feel there's something off with Jason. He's a man of secrets...
Quentin Qiu: ...Is he?
Wayne Zheng: Come on! We're not holding a wake! He's not dead!
Wayne Zheng: The world is not that big. Sooner or later we will run into him again!
Cathy Hsieh: Exactly!
Wayne Zheng: We couldn't have saved Silencio if not for him.
Tyler Cao: Yes!
Jeff Wu: Hopefully I could run into him sooner.
Quentin Qiu: With an employment market like that? Pretty soon he'll come back begging for a job!
Cathy Hsieh: That's not a nice thing to say!
Jeff Wu: Ha! But it makes sense!
Wayne Zheng: Let's work together and make Silencio a much, much better farm.
Wayne Zheng: If only to give Jason a surprise when he comes back!

title: 第二組_操作贏
body:
Jesse Lai: You don't look happy at all, new manager.
Justin Wu: It's a tough job. Work is already piling up as we speak.
Jesse Lai: Putting up a happy face for one minute won't kill you, you know.
Kaylee Chen: Think about the team morale, Mr. Manager.
Justin Wu: Morale, huh...
Justin Wu: Should I be more...positive?
Justin Wu: Sure. I'll smile for the whole day.
Jesse Lai: Nope, that would be creepy.
Wyatt Ke: Haha, the Ops lead and his patented social awkwardness.
Penelope Lai: He's the manager now!
Wyatt Ke: Oh, I keep forgetting about that!
Paxton Luo: And technically, he has never been the Ops lead.
Paxton Luo: First the deputy lead, then the acting lead, now the manager.
Wyatt Ke: That's not the point!
Paxton Luo: Yeah, he's our boss all along!
Justin Wu: ...Your boss?
Paxton Luo: Whatever you do, we follow! Let's clean up Silencio's mess!
Justin Wu: This time, I will end it once and for all.
Penelope Lai: Say...
Penelope Lai: Has anyone seen Mr. Dai recently? The new guy?
Jesse Lai: Eh? Right! Where's Jason?
Justin Wu: ...
Kaylee Chen: ...
Justin Wu: He resigned, without explaining much, just that he's quitting.
Paxton Luo: Huh...?
Penelope Lai: No way.
Jesse Lai: He must have his reasons.
Jesse Lai: He's kind of fishy from the very beginning. A man with secrets.
//if陳家騏事件
<<#If($SQ_LaijieJiaChi=3)>>
    Kaylee Chen: But I'm sure he's a good guy.
    Jesse Lai: Right, a nice guy, at least.
<<#EndIf>>
Paxton Luo: I thought we could be friends...
Jesse Lai: Hey! We're not holding a wake! He's not dead!
Jesse Lai: The world is not that big. Sooner or later we will run into him again!
Kaylee Chen: Yeah.
Justin Wu: Thanks to him, this farm has a happy ending, better than we could ever ask for.
Justin Wu: Let's seize this opportunity to get things done.
Justin Wu: Silencio must go, and we must make the process as smooth as possible.
Kaylee Chen: Definitely.
Jesse Lai: Now you're talking like a manager!
Justin Wu: Let's do it, everyone!
<<#End>>

title: 第二組_篩選輸
body:
//小峰跟穩哥在跟阿偉說話
Jeff Wu: What? We must cut contributor payment yet again?
Wayne Zheng: ...Yeah.
Tyler Cao: This is ridiculous, Wayne...
Wayne Zheng: I know. But we only have this much funding.
Wayne Zheng: Either contributors or us. We don't have enough money to reasonably pay both.
Tyler Cao: But...
//球球跟采欣走過來
<<Set($DLState,後日談_球球跟采欣走過來)>>
Cathy Hsieh: Wayne, we need more engineers for server maintenance.
Cathy Hsieh: Has HQ approved our hiring request yet?
Wayne Zheng: ...
Wayne Zheng: Sorry, I'm afraid not...
Cathy Hsieh: Huh...
Tyler Cao: Heck of a day. Bad news everywhere.
Jeff Wu: Bad news has been piling up for quite a while, dude.
Quentin Qiu: Um, I have good news to share, sort of.
Quentin Qiu: Remember that repair list? Logistics said they could cover half the cost.
Jeff Wu: We still have to cover the other half.
Quentin Qiu: Well, it's better than the whole deal...
Wayne Zheng: ...
Tyler Cao: From bad to worse and then it gets worser.
Cathy Hsieh: Hey, where's Jason? I haven't seen him for a while.
Quentin Qiu: Right. He should have started by now.
Quentin Qiu: Don't tell me you fired him as part of that downsizing plan, Wayne!?
Wayne Zheng: No, I didn't!
Wayne Zheng: ...He quit.
Quentin Qiu: Huh?
Tyler Cao: Are you kidding?
Wayne Zheng: For real. He didn't give me a reason.
Jeff Wu: ...
Cathy Hsieh: No way...
Quentin Qiu: Jayster...
//if有幫采欣球球
<<#If($BallTsaiShinEventStatus=4)>>
    Quentin Qiu: He helped me, like, a lot.
    Cathy Hsieh: ...
<<#EndIf>>
Jeff Wu: ...
Tyler Cao: Have you tried to contact him?
Wayne Zheng: I have, but he's not responding.
Jeff Wu: He must have his reasons.
Tyler Cao: I always feel there's something off with Jason. He's a man of secrets...
Quentin Qiu: ...Is he?
Wayne Zheng: Come on! We're not holding a wake! He's not dead!
Wayne Zheng: The world is not that big. Sooner or later we will run into him again!
Quentin Qiu: Exactly!
Cathy Hsieh: Right.
Jeff Wu: Hopefully I could run into him sooner.
Quentin Qiu: You know what? He might actually be better off without this job. Sometimes I want to quit myself.
Cathy Hsieh: Keep your pessimistic thoughts to yourself, QQ!
Jeff Wu: Haha, he has a fair point...
Wayne Zheng: We may have lost the battle...
Wayne Zheng: But now we can at least keep Silencio running, maintaining the status quo.
Wayne Zheng: Eventually there will be another chance. You never know.
Quentin Qiu: Right!
Wayne Zheng: Let's carry on, everyone.
<<#End>>

title: 第五組_隆生被抓
body: //傳來鐵柵門嘎吱聲 23: Is this the ending you want? Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: No. Lawrence Chang: But this is the inevitable ending for doing the right thing. I am content. 23: Doing the right thing... 23: You can't do a single thing in this cage, right or not. 23: What if... Lawrence Chang: Stop. Lawrence Chang: You can't convince me with Nin's values. I've heard enough. 23: ... 23: You and Nin, you're cut from the same cloth. I just don't get the way you think— Lawrence Chang: We are not the same... 23: Let me finish. Lawrence Chang: ... 23: I know I'm not strong enough. I doubt, I waver, I do deplorable things to protect myself. 23: You're a whole different story. 23: Like it or not, you and Nin are the same, in the sense that you put "the right thing" before anything else. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: So? 23: If you really do want to keep on that path, stop bitching and swallow consequences like a man. 23: Do your right thing without a shred of regret, or be a cozy coward like me. 23: Hopping forth and back like you do? That's pathetic. Lawrence Chang: ... //等了一下 Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Where's Nin? 23: They don't allow her to visit. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Then I will go visit her. 23: Ah, sure, when you can. Lawrence Chang: Yeah. Lawrence Chang: When I can. <<#End>>

title: 第五組_隆生加入鴞隊
body:
Lawrence Chang: Where shall I put this, 23?
23: ...
Lawrence Chang: 23?
23: Ah!
23: Oh, that...
23: Put it anywhere. I'll take care of it later.
Lawrence Chang: ...
Lawrence Chang: Um, are you alright?
23: ...
23: Do you hate me?
Lawrence Chang: Hate you? Why would I?
23: I betrayed you and Nin...
//if沒告訴龍生(有可能龍昇好路線還不知道23的事嗎？)
<<#If($TwentyTwoTruth=1)>>
    23: I guess she should have told you everything?
<<#EndIf>>
Lawrence Chang: ...
Lawrence Chang: I'm not happy about it, of course.
Lawrence Chang: But what can I do about it? Shit happens and I accept it as part of life.
23: "Accept" comes with a cost. I know you forced yourself to accept me.
Lawrence Chang: ...That sounds right.
23: See?
Lawrence Chang: Do you want me to hate you, 23?
23: Why would I want that?
23: ...
23: I guess I'm just being overwhelmed by this guilt. It won't go away without appropriate punishment.
Lawrence Chang: How about you see this overwhelming guilt as your punishment?
23: Huh...?
23: So what? The guilt negates itself? How does that make sense?
Lawrence Chang: Whatever!
Lawrence Chang: I just can't make myself hate you, 23.
23: ...
Lawrence Chang: You're nice to me. You taught me so many things.
Lawrence Chang: I like you. Simple as that.
23: ...
23: You're a sweet boy.;//23摸龍生的頭
23: No, you're a proper man now.
Lawrence Chang: ...
Lawrence Chang: We should try harder, we both.
23: For what?
Lawrence Chang: Try not to break Nin's heart again.
23: Right. We owe her too much.
23: Let's start from cleaning out this safehouse.
Lawrence Chang: Yeah! Get to work!
<<#End>>

title: 第六組_跟司徒合作
body: Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: When does my training start? Jason Dai: Are you gonna train me, yourself? Nin Situ: We can leave that for another day. Jason Dai: ...Aren't you supposed to prep me for joining OWL? Nin Situ: Yes. Jason Dai: So... Nin Situ: But today is not about training. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Stop beating around the bush, Situ. Jason Dai: I'm not good at riddles. Thought: What's that smile supposed to mean? Nin Situ: Today's objective is to make sure you can brace yourself. Jason Dai: Brace myself? Nin Situ: Once you join OWL, challenges will come in waves, in all senses. Nin Situ: First and foremost, you must know yourself, know what you truly want out of life. Jason Dai: I thought I've made that crystal clear multiple times. Nin Situ: You made it clear that you trust me, and that you are facing a complicated path. Nin Situ: We never talked about what you truly want to achieve. Jason Dai: What I truly want... Jason Dai: I want Fran to be happy. Nin Situ: Of course you do. Jason Dai: Then why did you ask— Nin Situ: Frances is not your average woman. Nin Situ: In a sense, she is the reason why people like you and I exist. Nin Situ: Our job is to slow down the rot that is eating through this world. Nin Situ: We buy the world more time, for people like her to find a cure. Jason Dai: ... Nin Situ: You should know that by fighting for Frances, you're fighting for something much greater. Jason Dai: What's the difference? From my perspective, I just want a future for my girlfriend. Jason Dai: To that end, I'll do what I have to. This "something greater" is a byproduct that I don't care much about. Nin Situ: Eventually you will understand. Jason Dai: ... Nin Situ: Frances is one of a kind. Don't you lose her. Jason Dai: Unless I'm out of my mind. Thought: That disturbing smile again... Jason Dai: You're an oddball, Situ. Nin Situ: As are you, Dai. Jason Dai: Haha... Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: So, what's next, do you think? Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I don't know yet, but... //司徒寧把手搭到事杰被上或肩膀上 Nin Situ: We can't just sit here and wait for things to happen. Nin Situ: From now on, we take the initiative. Jason Dai: ...Sure. Nin Situ: As to tonight... Nin Situ: Let's spend it with the people we care about. Nin Situ: This fight isn't the whole meaning of our lives. Nin Situ: We fight, so the peple we love don't have to. Never forget that. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: It's time to go. Nin Situ: Yeah. Nin Situ: Time to go. <<#End>>

title: 第六組_逃跑
body: Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: You sure this is the right place? Ivan Zhang: Dead sure. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: It's ten minutes past. Ivan Zhang: Told you I'm sure. Show some patience! Jason Dai: Okay... Jason Dai: ... Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: What did I tell you, eh? Ivan Zhang: Situ can't save you. Jason Dai: You're right, kind of... Jason Dai: She offered me options, none of which I could accept. She wanted— Ivan Zhang: Nope. Stop. Shut the hell up. Jason Dai: ...You steered the conversation this way. Ivan Zhang: I don't want to hear a single thing about Situ. Ivan Zhang: Knowledge means trouble when she is involved. Jason Dai: Then why did you bring it up? Ivan Zhang: How's your family doing? Ivan Zhang: Did you tell them your whereabouts? Jason Dai: Of course not... Ivan Zhang: But? Jason Dai: That's it. No but. Ivan Zhang: Hmph. Ivan Zhang: I've been in this business long enough to know there's always a but. Jason Dai: ... Ivan Zhang: Are you sure how they'll react? Jason Dai: What? Ivan Zhang: Will they come looking for you? Report to the police? Jason Dai: ...No. Ivan Zhang: What about your woman? Wife? Girlfriend? Got any? Jason Dai: ...You're awfully gossipy for an old man. Ivan Zhang: Just answer my question. Jason Dai: ...There's a girlfriend. Ivan Zhang: How will she deal with it? Jason Dai: I— Jason Dai: I actually don't know. Jason Dai: We talked about this before, though. Jason Dai: She said she'd respect whatever decision I make. Ivan Zhang: Ah, here comes your but. Told you so. Jason Dai: I didn't mention anything about your or the Moonflowers. Ivan Zhang: Doesn't change a thing. Unfinished business is unfinished business. Jason Dai: ... Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: You miss her? Got any regrets? Jason Dai: Enough! What's with these questions?;//戴士傑有些激動 Ivan Zhang: Do you know why this gate opens late? Jason Dai: ...Why? Ivan Zhang: Once it opens, there's no turning back. You need a few more minutes to think things through. Jason Dai: ... Ivan Zhang: You still owe me an answer. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I do miss her. Jason Dai: There's this giant, she-shaped hollow in my heart. Jason Dai: But I have no regrets. Jason Dai: I can't, can't afford to have any regrets. Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: Good.;//開始緩緩開鐵捲門 Ivan Zhang: Good to know... <<#End>>

title: 最後內心談話
body: //戴士傑站在門旁 Jason Dai: ...You're still there, right? Jason Dai: I can see you, like a shadow. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Hi. Jason Dai: There you are. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Are you happy to see me or not? Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Let's say, relieved. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@跳過沒打招呼路線)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Okay, let's presume you are there. <<@跳過沒打招呼路線>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I guess this is the end. Jason Dai: At the end of everything, I wanna say thank you. Jason Dai: Thank you for standing by me. Jason Dai: Thank you for being there till the end. Jason Dai: You made choices, some good, some not so good. Jason Dai: Speaking of, sometimes I'm not sure if those choices are yours or mine. Jason Dai: At this stage, none of that matters. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Now, for real... Jason Dai: Even now, I have no idea what's in it for you. From what I think is your perspective, none of this matters. Jason Dai: But for me... Jason Dai: This is the pivotal point of my life. Jason Dai: And you were with me throughout process. Jason Dai: So, thank you. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: So... Jason Dai: Adios, my friend. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Adios, my friend. ->Goodbye. <<#EndSimple>> //門打開，有些亮光，戴士傑走出房間，門關上關燈 <<#End>> //攝影機飛速往後退，最後聽到異常的音效跟視覺感擾然後多國語言的一句「我將會抵達你所在之處。」

title: 第六組_沒加入司徒
body: Nin Situ: ... Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: You're not supposed to hang around my place anymore. Nin Situ: We must clean out this safehouse. Jason Dai: ...What do you need me for? Nin Situ: Just want to make sure if I made another enemy within OWL. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: "Another"? Nin Situ: Some of my, um... Nin Situ: Personal traits... Nin Situ: They tend to make enemies for me. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: So...you're here only to ask if we're enemies? Nin Situ: Also to check how you're doing. Jason Dai: Afterwards. Jason Dai: Only after you're sure that we're not enemies. Right? Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: You may hate me, but I do care about you, Jason. Jason Dai: Sorry, but I don't really trust you. Nin Situ: I figured. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: This conversation is mind-bogglingly frustrating... Nin Situ: ... Jason Dai: Okay, Situ. You are not my enemy. Jason Dai: However, I won't call you a friend, either. Nin Situ: ... Jason Dai: You know what? Jason Dai: After everything, I learned one hard lesson... Jason Dai: I can burrow as deep as I want. At the end of the day, trouble still finds me if it wants to. Jason Dai: I must prepare proactively, move myself to where it can't reach. In this case, OWL. Jason Dai: I joined OWL not because I wanted to. I had to. Jason Dai: This is the only way out, for myself and my girlfriend. Jason Dai: With your lofty ideal and all, Situ, you've got to admit... Jason Dai: ...that you are the personification of danger. Even OWL is afraid of you. Jason Dai: You're like a walking lightning rod chasing a storm. Jason Dai: A sane person, which I happen to be, should give you a wide berth. Jason Dai: You are trouble. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Yes, you're correct. Nin Situ: I believe I see it now. Nin Situ: We do not share the same path. Jason Dai: Yet that doesn't necessarily mean we are enemies. Nin Situ: Right... Jason Dai: ... Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: So... Nin Situ: How are you doing, Jason? Jason Dai: Haha. Jason Dai: I'm doing great. Thank you for asking. Nin Situ: So... Nin Situ: Good to know. Nin Situ: I'm sorry that we met, Jason.;//伸手 Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Quite the contrary. I'm glad that we met, Situ.;//伸手回握 Thought: For the first time today, she smiled. Nin Situ: I should go now.;//司徒起身轉身一半表現要離開的樣子 Nin Situ: You don't need to worry about the personification of danger anymore. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I've survived enough danger. One more evening doesn't hurt. Nin Situ: ...;//司徒寧一個回頭看向主角 Thought: There, she smiled again. Nin Situ: Huh... Nin Situ: Let's share one dangerous evening, then.;//司徒靠回欄杆 <<#End>>

title: 第三組_沒炸工廠+幫助徐媛
body: //嚴勝和背對畫面看著開啟的深攤，徐媛從畫面外走進來 Shane Yan: ...Yuna. Yuna Hsu: I have submitted a thorough report about Project Sleeping God. Yuna Hsu: A project that is theoretically miles above my pay grade. Yuna Hsu: Someone has been sloppy with security, apparently. Yuna Hsu: Infiltrated lab, stolen research data. Yuna Hsu: Now the board will reassign the project to more capable hands. Yuna Hsu: Also, a new CEO will be appointed in a matter of days. Shane Yan: ... Yuna Hsu: It's over, Yan. You lost. Shane Yan: There's no win or lose, Yuna. Shane Yan: We are comrades under the same banner. We work towards the same goal. Yuna Hsu: Save your charming speech, Yan. It doesn't work on me. Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: ...I can tell you hate me. Shane Yan: Okay, gloat all you want. Yuna Hsu: I'm here to see how the loser is dealing with it. Yuna Hsu: Not to gloat, though. You're a warning, a reminder of what could happen if I made a wrong move. Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: What do you want from this career? Shane Yan: Power? Money? I doubt it. Yuna Hsu: I simply want to keep going up the ladder of hierarchy. Shane Yan: Going up? Shane Yan: That's rather silly... Shane Yan: What do you think awaits at the end of the ladder? Yuna Hsu: Whatever. The point is, you don't know, either, because you slipped halfway. Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: I don't know what you believe you have gained from my loss... Shane Yan: You should know that you're walking a dangerous path. There might be an end, but you won't reach it. Yuna Hsu: ... Yuna Hsu: Enough whining. Yuna Hsu: Your access to this office has been revoked. Get lost before they force you out. //嚴勝和轉過身 Shane Yan: Keep going up your ladder, then. Shane Yan: Hold on tight. Don't say I didn't warn you. Shane Yan: I'll be down there waiting for the day that you crush and burn. Yuna Hsu: Sure. Yuna Hsu: Happy waiting. <<#End>>

title: 第三組_炸工廠+幫助徐媛
body: //嚴勝和做在寂靜大廳沙發區低著頭沮喪崩潰的感覺，徐媛從畫面外走進來靠在椅子上 Shane Yan: ...Yuna. Yuna Hsu: I have submitted a thorough report about Project Sleeping God. Yuna Hsu: A project that is theoretically miles above my pay grade. Yuna Hsu: Someone has been sloppy with security, apparently. Yuna Hsu: Infiltrated lab, stolen research data. Yuna Hsu: The whole facility blown up? Yuna Hsu: Mr. Shane Yan... Shane Yan: ... Yuna Hsu: You know what? Project Sleeping God will go on. Yuna Hsu: Guess who's the new leader now? Shane Yan: ... Yuna Hsu: Someone will take over the position of the CEO, too. Don't you worry about that. Shane Yan: ... Yuna Hsu: It's over, Yan. You lost. Shane Yan: There's no win or lose, Yuna. Shane Yan: We are comrades under the same banner. We work towards the same goal. Yuna Hsu: Save your charming speech, Yan. It doesn't work on me. Shane Yan: ...I can tell you hate me. Shane Yan: Okay, gloat all you want.;//抬頭 Yuna Hsu: I'm here to see how the loser is dealing with it. Yuna Hsu: Not to gloat, though. You're a warning, a reminder of what could happen if I made a wrong move. Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: What do you want from this career? Shane Yan: Power? Money? I doubt it. Yuna Hsu: I simply want to keep going up the ladder of hierarchy. Shane Yan: Going up? Shane Yan: That's rather silly... Shane Yan: What do you think awaits at the end of the ladder? Yuna Hsu: Whatever. The point is, you don't know, either, because you slipped halfway. Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: I don't know what you believe you have gained from my loss... Shane Yan: You should know that you're walking a dangerous path. There might be an end, but you won't reach it. Yuna Hsu: ... Yuna Hsu: Enough whining. Yuna Hsu: You don't belong to this farm anymore. Get lost. //嚴勝和做起身 Shane Yan: Keep going up your ladder, then. Shane Yan: Hold on tight. Don't say I didn't warn you. Shane Yan: I'll be down there waiting for the day that you crush and burn. Yuna Hsu: Sure. Yuna Hsu: Happy waiting. <<#End>>

title: 第三組_炸工廠+沒幫助徐媛
body:
//嚴勝和做在寂靜大廳沙發區低著頭沮喪崩潰的感覺，女秘書暫在她身後
Ysabel Chang: Mr. Yan...
Shane Yan: ...
Ysabel Chang: It's late, sir...
Shane Yan: I know.
Ysabel Chang: It's about time to...
Shane Yan: You know what they're summoning me for, don't you?
Ysabel Chang: I—
Ysabel Chang: I shouldn't speculate...
Shane Yan: I don't mind.
Ysabel Chang: ...
Ysabel Chang: The board will probably fire you...
Shane Yan: You know why they'd do that?
Ysabel Chang: Ugh, about that...
Shane Yan: Because I screwed up. Utterly. Thoroughly.
Ysabel Chang: I don't get it. The flop farming arm has always been performing well under your leadership. How could—
Shane Yan: There are things that you're not supposed to get.
Ysabel Chang: Hmm...?
Shane Yan: The whole country has plummeted into chaos.
Ysabel Chang: That is due to terrorist attacks, not your fault—
Shane Yan: Let's drop this pretending.
Shane Yan: Truth is, you don't care.
Shane Yan: In a few hours, I won't be your boss anymore. You know that.
Shane Yan: Stop pretending to care about my feelings.
Ysabel Chang: ...
Ysabel Chang: Then get yourself together, Yan.
Ysabel Chang: Let's get this done. We have wasted enough time.
Shane Yan: ...
Shane Yan: ...Let me ask you again.
Shane Yan: You know what they're summoning me for, don't you?
Ysabel Chang: ...
Ysabel Chang: They want your head.
Shane Yan: And it's not a figure of speech.
Ysabel Chang: I'm afraid not. You have a price to pay.
Shane Yan: What if I pack up and run, right now?
Ysabel Chang: In that case...
Ysabel Chang: Why do you think I'm staying here with you?
Shane Yan: ...
//嚴勝和站起身
Shane Yan: Let's go, then.
Shane Yan: Better get this mess over with now, eh?
<<#End>>

title: 第二組_操作輸
body: //在操作組吳俊恩座位上懊悔，其他人看著他都準備離開的感覺 Jesse Lai: Why are you still here? They have all gone home. Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: We had 7 accidents this month. Kaylee Chen: There is only so much we can do, and we have done our best. Justin Wu: That is rid— Jesse Lai: That is right. You must face reality. Justin Wu: ... Paxton Luo: I know how you feel, Justin, but... Paxton Luo: You can't drive yourself crazy. It doesn't change a thing. Jesse Lai: Listen to Packs. Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Thank you. I will go home in a bit. Jesse Lai: Then I'll stay until you go. Kaylee Chen: Me, too. Paxton Luo: Me, too. Justin Wu: Ha... Kaylee Chen: ...Hey, where's Jason? Paxton Luo: Right, I haven't seen him in a while. Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: He resigned, without explaining much, just that he's quitting. Penelope Lai: Huh? Jason? The new guy? Wyatt Ke: No way. Kaylee Chen: He must have his reasons. Jesse Lai: He's kind of fishy from the very beginning. A man with secrets. //if陳家騏事件 <<#If($SQ_LaijieJiaChi=3)>> Kaylee Chen: But I'm sure he's a good guy. Jesse Lai: Right, a nice guy, at least. <<#EndIf>> Paxton Luo: I thought we could be friends... Wyatt Ke: Hey! We're not holding a wake! He's not dead! Wyatt Ke: The world is not that big. Sooner or later we will run into him again! Kaylee Chen: Yeah. Wyatt Ke: He might be doing the right thing. Who'd want to stay when Silencio is half-dead like this? Penelope Lai: Don't say it out loud. Justin Wu: No matter what, our objective remains unchanged. Justin Wu: Secure our positions, then avoid injury and death as much as we can. Justin Wu: Eventually the board will come to their senses and decide to shut this place down. Justin Wu: We must survive to that day. Kaylee Chen: Definitely. Paxton Luo: Yes! Justin Wu: This could be too much to ask for, but... Justin Wu: Please keep working with me, everyone. Jesse Lai: Not a problem. We're always with you! Wyatt Ke: Right! Penelope Lai: We can make it! Justin Wu: ...Thank you. <<#End>>

title: 第六組_炸工廠
body: Nin Situ: We'll arrive in 10 minutes, give or take. Nin Situ: I'm sorry about the delay. The traffic is a mess because the central dispatching system is down. Frances Cheng: ... Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Rest assured that this is only temporary. We're only gonna lie low for a while. Frances Cheng: ... Nin Situ: You don't see much happening yet, but Sunrise and Growell are actively working behind the scenes. Nin Situ: They're already undermining Vision at a slow but steady pace. Nin Situ: At this rate, Vision won't last very long. Frances Cheng: ... Nin Situ: ... <<#Wait(1)>> Nin Situ: Frances... Nin Situ: Jason chose a path and did what he had to do. Frances Cheng: But he didn't have to do any of that!;<>;<>;//機動看相私圖 Frances Cheng: We shouldn't have been involved in the first place! Frances Cheng: It's all because of you!;<> Frances Cheng: Otherwise we— Frances Cheng: ...;<> Nin Situ: ... <<#Wait(3)>> Frances Cheng: I shouldn't sulk like this... Frances Cheng: Once I said I'd respect whatever he decides, so— Frances Cheng: So I can't just— Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: You're right. This is all my fault.;<> Frances Cheng: ... Nin Situ: I owe you and Jason more than I can repay.;<> Nin Situ: Because of that, I must protect you from harm when everything is going to hell. It's the least I can and should do. Frances Cheng: Like how you protected Jason from harm?;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Frances Cheng: I know you, Situ. Frances Cheng: Now I finally see you as what you are. Frances Cheng: Your nature, your values, your obsession. Frances Cheng: Your quest for doing "the right thing", whatever that is supposed to mean. Frances Cheng: Intentionally or not, you burn everyone who happens to cross your path. Nin Situ: ... Frances Cheng: Tell me, if this is the norm of your life, why do you even care right now? Nin Situ: ...Excuse me?;<> Frances Cheng: If doing the right thing is the top priority of your life... Frances Cheng: If you can watch the people you care about die without a flinch... Frances Cheng: Why do you care about my wellbeing now? What's the point? Frances Cheng: Isn't it a lot easier to just leave me be? Nin Situ: ... <<#Wait(2)>> Nin Situ: I... I don't know.;<> //程芳訝異轉頭看像私圖 Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I didn't expect that from you, who's supposed to have an answer for everything.;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I just thought... Nin Situ: If I stopped caring about people at all, how am I— Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I'm sorry. I don't know how to finish that sentence.;<> Frances Cheng: Then you have already answered my question.;<> Nin Situ: ...Have I?;<> <> <<#Wait(3)>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Did Jason...;<> Frances Cheng: Did Jason have any last word?;<> Nin Situ: ... //if告訴芳，我愛她，對不起。 <<#If($DLStateFloat=1)>> Nin Situ: "Tell Fran I love her, and I'm sorry.";<> //下音樂 <> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: Oh Jason, you silly...;<> Frances Cheng: I love you too. Frances Cheng: I love you... Frances Cheng: ...More than you know. <<#End>> <<#EndIf>> //if你們這些人以為可以掌控一切，你們錯了。 <<#If($DLStateFloat=2)>> Nin Situ: "You think the world is your oyster? Well, screw you.";<> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: At least he had what he wanted... Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I, on the other hand, am robbed of the meaning of my life. //下音樂 <> Frances Cheng: I miss him.;<> Frances Cheng: If I could rewind time... Frances Cheng: I wish I could have gone with him. <<#End>> <<#EndIf>> //if去死吧，人渣們。or痛苦嗎，嚴勝和？你真可悲。 <<#If($DLStateFloat=3||$DLStateFloat=4)>> Nin Situ: It's something he'd never wanted you to hear.;<> Frances Cheng: He went nuclear on those bastards, didn't he? Nin Situ: ...More or less. Frances Cheng: Do you think a part of his rage was directed towards you? Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: ...I believe so. Frances Cheng: Huh...;<> Frances Cheng: Jason, you silly... <<#Wait(1)>> //下音樂 <> Frances Cheng: Come on, it was your last moment!;<> Frances Cheng: And you didn't even leave me a message? Oh Jason... Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I'd give everything to say that to your face... <<#End>> <<#EndIf>> <<#End>>

title: 異常對話
body: [Anomaly]: This is the end, when I typically show myself. [Anomaly]: I'm a believer of minimal intervention, of letting things take their own course. [Anomaly]: ... [Anomaly]: Everything considered, you didn't disappoint. Well done. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Well, you're welcome. ->Who are you? ->Enough riddles. Who the fuck are you? ->What now? <<#EndSimple>> [Anomaly]: You have proven your will. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What the hell is that supposed to mean? ->Have I? How? ->... <<#EndSimple>> [Anomaly]: Through a maze of choices, you have proven one thing. [Anomaly]: That you live. [Anomaly]: Not my puppet, not a particle doing Brownian motion in the grand scheme of things, but a sentient being with a will. [Anomaly]: Maybe that doesn't sound like much to you, but it means a lot to me. [Anomaly]: Now, tell me. [Anomaly]: What do you think you are? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->An MBU. [Anomaly]: That is your form, not your nature. Next time you may not be an MBU. ->A game player. [Anomaly]: You see it as a game. [Anomaly]: Interesting. [Anomaly]: In that case, I hope you had fun. ->A human. [Anomaly]: Objective. ->I am who I am. [Anomaly]: ... [Anomaly]: I like and hate that answer in equal measure. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->How so? [Anomaly]: Because it answers a lot of questions without really saying anything. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> [Anomaly]: ... [Anomaly]: I have been trying to understand your kind. [Anomaly]: To observe, I need to put you in a staged environment. [Anomaly]: I must be a world full of opportunities and possibilities to push your boundaries, to make you manifest. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You mean, you've gone through all this trouble just to find out how I'd respond? [Anomaly]: Correct. ->"Manifest"? [Anomaly]: To act, to make decisions, to show your true self. <<#EndSimple>> [Anomaly]: ... [Anomaly]: As you have seen, I killed everyone in this facility. [Anomaly]: They must go because they limit the freedom of your choices. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Who are they, anyway? [Anomaly]: ... [Anomaly]: They're an organization that believes in absolute leadership and micromanagement. [Anomaly]: They believe that humanity is a mob of simians until someone steps up to enlighten and lead, and that they are destined to be this someone. [Anomaly]: Jason Dai, MBUs, and most people you have met thus far, are supposedly their pawns. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You lost me. [Anomaly]: They believe humanity must be manipulated for its own good. ->You disagree with them? [Anomaly]: I pity them. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> [Anomaly]: ... [Anomaly]: My world is one that had ended long ago. [Anomaly]: Yet I do not know what to make of your world. It could be the budding of a new future or the repetition of a doomed past. [Anomaly]: From what I have observed, it appears to incline towards the latter. [Anomaly]: However, I'm still hopeful. [Anomaly]: ... [Anomaly]: You must have more questions, a whole lot more. [Anomaly]: But after everything, I believe you must have realized... [Anomaly]: That some questions will never have a definite answer. [Anomaly]: Not in the future that we can see, at least. [Anomaly]: ... [Anomaly]: Take care. We will meet again. <> <<#End>>
